Question: What processor does the ECU-750 use?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 uses a 32-bit Cortex-M4 processor running at 120 MHz. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: What is the operating voltage range for the ECU-750?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 operates at a voltage range of 9V to 16V. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: What connectors are available on the ECU-750?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 has two connectors: one main automotive connector and one JTAG connector. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: What is the peak power consumption of the ECU-750?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 has a peak power consumption of 500mA, with a typical consumption of 150mA. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: What safety certification does the ECU-750 hold?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 is certified for ISO 26262 ASIL-B. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: How does the ECU-750 provide diagnostic information?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 provides basic diagnostic trouble codes (DTCs) via the CAN bus interface. For a full list of codes, refer to document DOC-700-DIAG. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: How much RAM does the ECU-750 have?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 has 512 KB of SRAM. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: Is the ECU-700 series still actively developed?
Expected_Answer: No. The ECU-700 Series is now in legacy support and has been succeeded by the ECU-800 Series. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: What processor does the ECU-850 use?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 uses a dual-core ARM Cortex-A53 processor running at 1.2 GHz. (source: ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: What type of storage does the ECU-850 have and how much?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 has 16 GB of eMMC storage. (source: ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: Does the ECU-850 have an Ethernet interface?
Expected_Answer: Yes, the ECU-850 has one 100BASE-T1 Ethernet interface. (source: ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: What operating system does the ECU-850 run?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 runs a Yocto-based Linux OS. (source: ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: How are applications deployed on the ECU-850?
Expected_Answer: Application deployment on the ECU-850 is managed via Docker containers. (source: ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: What is the idle power consumption of the ECU-850?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 consumes 500mA when idle. (source: ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: Does the ECU-850 support secure boot?
Expected_Answer: Yes, the ECU-850 supports Secure Boot and includes a Hardware Security Module (HSM). (source: ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: What is the power consumption of the ECU-850 under load?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 consumes 1.5A under load and 500mA when idle. (source: ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: What is the clock speed of the ECU-850b processor?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850b has dual-core ARM Cortex-A53 cores clocked at 1.5 GHz, which is an upgrade from the base ECU-850's 1.2 GHz. (source: ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: How much storage does the ECU-850b have?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850b has 32 GB of eMMC storage. (source: ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: What is the idle power consumption of the ECU-850b?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850b consumes 550mA when idle. (source: ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: What is the NPU performance rating of the ECU-850b?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850b has a dedicated Neural Processing Unit (NPU) capable of 5 TOPS (Tera Operations Per Second). (source: ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: Does the ECU-850b have an Ethernet interface?
Expected_Answer: Yes, the ECU-850b has one 100BASE-T1 Ethernet interface, the same as the base ECU-850. (source: ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: What is the CAN bus specification of the ECU-850b?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850b features a dual channel CAN FD interface with speeds up to 2 Mbps per channel. (source: ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: Compare the RAM between ECU-850 and ECU-850b.
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 has 2 GB of LPDDR4 RAM, while the ECU-850b has an upgraded 4 GB of LPDDR4 RAM, doubling the memory capacity to support larger AI models. (sources: ECU-800_Series_Base.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: Compare the storage capacity of ECU-850 and ECU-850b.
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 has 16 GB of eMMC storage, while the ECU-850b doubles this to 32 GB of eMMC storage. (sources: ECU-800_Series_Base.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: How does the power consumption differ between ECU-850 and ECU-850b?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 consumes 500mA idle and 1.5A under load, while the ECU-850b consumes slightly more at 550mA idle and 1.7A under load, reflecting the additional power draw from the NPU and higher clock speed. (sources: ECU-800_Series_Base.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: Compare the processor clock speeds of ECU-850 and ECU-850b.
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 has dual-core ARM Cortex-A53 cores running at 1.2 GHz, while the ECU-850b runs the same cores at a higher 1.5 GHz clock speed. (sources: ECU-800_Series_Base.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: Compare the processors used in the ECU-750 and ECU-850.
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 uses a 32-bit Cortex-M4 processor at 120 MHz, a microcontroller-class chip, while the ECU-850 uses a much more powerful dual-core ARM Cortex-A53 application processor at 1.2 GHz. The ECU-850 represents a major generational leap in processing capability. (sources: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md, ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: Compare the memory capacity of ECU-750 and ECU-850b.
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 has 512 KB of SRAM, while the ECU-850b has 4 GB of LPDDR4 RAM. The ECU-850b has roughly 8000 times more memory than the ECU-750, reflecting its role as an advanced platform for AI workloads. (sources: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: Compare the power consumption of ECU-750 and ECU-850.
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 has a typical consumption of 150mA and a peak of 500mA, while the ECU-850 consumes 500mA at idle and 1.5A under load. The ECU-850 draws significantly more power, consistent with its more powerful processor and richer feature set. (sources: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md, ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: Which ECU models have Ethernet connectivity?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 and ECU-850b (ECU-800 Series) both have a 100BASE-T1 Ethernet interface. The ECU-750 (ECU-700 Series) does not have Ethernet connectivity. (sources: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md, ECU-800_Series_Base.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: Compare the security features across ECU-750 and ECU-850.
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 is certified for ISO 26262 ASIL-B but does not mention secure boot or HSM capabilities. The ECU-850 supports Secure Boot and includes a Hardware Security Module (HSM), offering a more comprehensive security architecture. (sources: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md, ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: Which ECU models support Docker container deployment?
Expected_Answer: Docker container deployment is supported on the ECU-850, which runs a Yocto-based Linux OS. The ECU-850b, being an enhanced variant of the ECU-850, inherits this capability. The ECU-750 does not support Docker. (sources: ECU-800_Series_Base.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: Which ECU models have a Hardware Security Module?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 and ECU-850b (ECU-800 Series) include a Hardware Security Module (HSM). The ECU-750 (ECU-700 Series) does not mention HSM support. (sources: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md, ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: Which ECU models have a dedicated NPU?
Expected_Answer: Only the ECU-850b has a dedicated Neural Processing Unit (NPU), capable of 5 TOPS. The base ECU-850 mentions AI/ML acceleration but refers to the ECU-850b for the enhanced NPU. The ECU-750 has no NPU or AI capabilities. (sources: ECU-800_Series_Base.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: Which ECU models support CAN FD?
Expected_Answer: All three ECU models support CAN FD. The ECU-750 has a single channel CAN FD interface at up to 1 Mbps, while the ECU-850 and ECU-850b both have dual channel CAN FD interfaces at up to 2 Mbps per channel. (sources: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md, ECU-800_Series_Base.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)

Question: What is the document ID for the ECU-700 series manual?
Expected_Answer: The document ID for the ECU-700 Series Product Manual is DOC-700-REV1.2. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: What diagnostic reference document should be consulted for ECU-750 DTCs?
Expected_Answer: For a full list of diagnostic trouble codes for the ECU-750, refer to document DOC-700-DIAG. (source: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md)

Question: What operating system does the ECU-850 use and how are apps managed?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-850 runs a Yocto-based Linux OS, and application deployment is managed via Docker containers. (source: ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: Compare the storage technologies used across the ECU-750 and ECU-850.
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 uses 2 MB of Internal Flash, a small embedded storage suitable for firmware, while the ECU-850 uses 16 GB of eMMC, a much larger and faster storage type suitable for running a full Linux OS and applications. The difference reflects the fundamental architectural shift from a microcontroller to an application processor. (sources: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md, ECU-800_Series_Base.md)

Question: Which ECU model has the lowest power consumption?
Expected_Answer: The ECU-750 has the lowest power consumption, with a typical draw of 150mA and a peak of 500mA. In comparison, the ECU-850 idles at 500mA and draws 1.5A under load, and the ECU-850b idles at 550mA and draws 1.7A under load. (sources: ECU-700_Series_Manual.md, ECU-800_Series_Base.md, ECU-800_Series_Plus.md)
